name: Maestro Relay
description: Bridge Discord and Slack bots to your Maestro agents, entirely inside Maestro. Base package: connections and config only — add an `agents:dispatch` permission naming your agent ids and re-sign before install to enable message routing (see plugin/README.md).
author: Maestro Relay contributors

permissions.reason: Hold the Discord Gateway websocket to receive messages.
Hold the Slack Socket Mode websocket. Host comes from apps.connections.open at runtime; the slack.com scope covers every wss-*.slack.com gateway (dot-boundary suffix match).
Call the Discord REST API (send messages, reactions, slash commands).
Download Discord attachments referenced in messages.
Call the Slack Web API (open Socket Mode, post messages, reactions).
List your agents so you can bind chat channels to them.
Read the bound agent's reply so it can be posted back to the chat channel.
Read bot tokens and the channel-to-agent registry.
Persist bot tokens and the channel-to-agent registry.
React to agent status changes for chat status indicators.
Surface bridge start/stop and connection errors.
Supervise and restart the gateway connections on crash.
Render the Maestro Relay configuration panel.
Show a relay status item in Maestro's UI.

settings.description: Minimum log level: debug | info | warn | error.
Discord guild (server) id for slash-command registration.
Comma-separated Discord user ids allowed to run commands (empty = all).
Comma-separated Slack user ids allowed to run commands (empty = all).

commands.title: Relay: Reload configuration
commands.description: Re-read tokens and settings, then reconnect.

commands.title: Relay: List rooms
commands.description: List multi-agent rooms and their masked personas.
